title: Cydon Prax
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=CIS |image=[[File:CydonPrax-WizardsCom.jpg]] |name=Cydon Prax |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] {{C|13:6 [[Great ReSynchronization|GrS]]}}, [[Thule/Legends|Thule]][[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]] |species=[[Chistori]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031211prax|text=Cydon Prax}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Cydon, activate the Harvester once I've taken off. When it has destroyed the forest, exterminate the remaining Wookiee population. I want no survivors.''"
"''Yes sir.|Dooku and Cydon Prax|}} '''Cydon Prax''' was a [[Chistori]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] employed by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] during the early [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of another [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], Prax took his place as the personal [[Soldier/Legends|enforcer]] of [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. In the [[Standard month/Legends|month]] that followed the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], Prax discovered the remains of the [[Force Harvester]]—an [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|ancient]] technology buried on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]]—with the help of the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Bera Kazan]]. Dooku had the weapon transported to the [[Kashyyyk system/Legends|Kashyyyk system]] and ordered Prax to conduct a field test on [[Alaris Prime/Legends|Alaris Prime]], a [[Forest/Legends|forest]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Homeworld|home]] to a local [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] population, where the Separatists could gauge the effects and magnitude of the Harvester's [[The Force/Legends|life force]] [[Force drain/Legends|draining]] power. Prax then joined the Separatists on the planet [[Thule/Legends|Thule]] where Dooku, satisfied by the experiment's [[Battle on Alaris|results]], converted the Harvester into a power source for the [[Dark Reaper]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. Recognizing the Dark Reaper as an [[Dark Reaper crisis|existential threat]] to both the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] personally oversaw the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic's]] [[Battle of Thule|invasion of Thule]]. During the battle, Dooku was evacuated while Prax confronted [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Jedi Commander]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] in the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[City/Legends|ruins]] of [[Kesiak]]. Despite Prax's efforts to kill the young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], the Chistori [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter was slain in battle, allowing Skywalker to complete the mission by destroying the Dark Reaper. ==Biography== {{Quote|Dispose of the Jedi.''"
"''My pleasure.|Dooku and Cydon Prax|}} [[File:Force_Harvester.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Cydon Prax unearthed the ancient Force Harvester with the help of Bera Kazan.]] A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Chistori]], Cydon Prax achieved distinction in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] as an elite [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]—the secessionist organization that waged a [[Clone Wars/Legends|civil war]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. With the [[Death/Legends|death]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|first engagement]] of the war, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]]—better known in public as [[Count/Legends|Count]] Dooku—had lost his most trusted right-hand [[Species/Legends|man]] to the [[Sai cha/Legends|beheading strike]] of the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu's]] [[Mace Windu's lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].{{Film|II}} In the same year that marked Fett's demise on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], Dooku swiftly replaced the late [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] with Cydon Prax. As Dooku's newest [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], Prax was entrusted with the execution of several missions that the Count regarded as important to the Separatist cause. Prax was also charged with taking custody over the young [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], an unmodified [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of Jango Fett that the latter raised as his son. However, Prax failed to be an effective substitute for the orphan's lost father. Cydon Prax accompanied Count Dooku to [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]] where the Separatists excavated the remains of the [[Force Harvester]], a vital component to the reconstruction of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|ancient]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] known as the [[Dark Reaper]]. A contingent of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] [[Conquest of Raxus Prime|attacked]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] and although the battle was ultimately a draw, both Dooku and Prax escaped with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] in pursuit. Skywalker confronted the Count alone in a brief [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] until Prax rendered him unconscious with the [[Stun/Legends|stun]] setting on his [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]]. Once [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] Harvester was fully reconstructed on [[Alaris Prime/Legends|Alaris Prime]], a forest [[Moon/Legends|moon]] that orbited the planet [[Alaris/Legends|Alaris]] in the [[Kashyyyk system/Legends|Kashyyyk system]], Cydon Prax activated the device on the order of Count Dooku, who wished to see its effects on [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-wielders]] like Skywalker. Prax then vacated the entire area soon after the Harvester's activation, given that it was designed to kill every living being within close proximity. Despite this, Skywalker managed to [[Battle on Alaris|escape]] the weapon's blast radius with the aid of [[Bera Kazan]], a former Separatist mercenary who aided Prax in locating the Harvester shortly before being betrayed and used as a test subject for the machine. [[File:Dooku_CIS.png|thumb|right|250px|Prax replaced the late Jango Fett as Count Dooku's bodyguard and enforcer.]] The [[Dark Reaper crisis|crisis]] came to a head at the [[Battle of Thule]], just as Count Dooku and Cydon Prax oversaw the completion of the new Dark Reaper superweapon. In the midst of a firefight between Republic [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] and Separatist [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], Prax entered the battlefield in his personal [[Dreadnought battle tank]]. Although he destroyed Mace Windu's [[TX-130 Saber-class fighter tank/Legends|TX-130 Saber-class fighter tank]], the Jedi Master survived his [[Tank/Legends|vessel's]] destruction and continued to lead the Republic forces on the front line. Meanwhile, Prax regrouped with Dooku in the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Kesiak]], also the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Thule/Legends|Thule]] where the Dark Reaper was held. When Anakin Skywalker led a [[Division/Legends|division]] of clone units into the city while piloting his own TX-130 tank, Count Dooku ordered Cydon Prax to confront and kill the young Jedi shortly before departing the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Thus, a firefight began between Prax and Skywalker that culminated with the latter's victory and the destruction of Prax's Dreadnaught battle tank, ending the Chistori mercenary's life. With the destruction of the Dark Reaper, the Republic claimed victory on Thule. ==Personality and traits== Cydon Prax was known as a callous, dispassionate being and a lethal soldier on the battlefield. His pitiless demeanor affected his ability to raise Boba Fett in Jango Fett's place, making him an unsuitable father figure. It also left him desensitized to mass murder as he felt no compunction about utilizing the Force Harvester's lethal energies on Alaris Prime, a moon filled with numerous specimens, including [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] settlers. His utter lack of ethics also rendered him unconcerned with acts of treachery, such as when he betrayed and [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] Bera Kazan shortly after she aided him in the Separatists' search for the Force Harvester. In addition to his skills as an elite [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] and marksman, Cydon Prax was a talented [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who could compete in battle with highly-skilled Jedi such as Mace Windu and Anakin Skywalker. Due to his Chistori biology, Prax was a cold-blooded reptilian; a fact that contributed to his reputation as a mercenary who possessed a "heart of ice." His other physical features included square teeth and a crown of horns that adorned the base of his skull.''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'' ==Equipment== [[File:Dreadnought.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Cydon Prax's Dreadnaught battle tank]] During the Clone Wars, Cydon Prax wore a fully-encased suit of [[Armor/Legends|armor]], much like his predecessor Jango Fett, that concealed his [[Alien/Legends|alien]] appearance. Though Prax's armor was not of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian design]], its most prominent feature was a left shoulder-mounted [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], which was most likely auto-targeting. Other assets in the armor's design included a system that maintained Prax's body temperature in a constant state in any environment. Due to his abilities as a pilot, Prax possessed a personal starfighter for aerial combat. However, his primary vehicle was a Dreadnaught battle tank that was heavily armed with [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], [[Mass-driver cannon/Legends|mass-driver cannons]], [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] and a [[plasma pulse discharger]]. ==Behind the scenes== Cydon Prax was voiced by [[André Sogliuzzo]] in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'']]. In ''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'', Prax is described as having a crown of horns and square teeth. No other known [[Chistori]] exhibits these traits. Cydon Prax also does not have a large snout like [[Desann]], given that the shape of his helmet reflects a [[Human/Legends|human]]-like skull. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]] {{1st}} *''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'' ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031211prax|text=Cydon Prax}} *{{FactFile|104|SUR 1-2|The Survival of Boba Fett}} *{{FactFile|107|SUR 3-6|The Survival of Boba Fett}} *{{FactFile|116|DOO 5-8|Count Dooku}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Cydon Prax }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Prax, Cydon}} [[Category:Bounty hunters]] [[Category:Chistori]] [[Category:Confederacy military personnel]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]]
